title: Introduce Virtualizer IIFE in docs/index.html
description: Add a vanilla-JS virtualization helper inside docs/index.html. Tracks viewport scroll position; renders only viewport + 5-card buffer above and below. No build step. No external library.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/index.html contains a module-level IIFE 'Virtualizer = (() => { ... })()' inside the existing <script> block.
Virtualizer.attach(containerEl, {itemHeight, totalCount, renderItem, onWindowChange}) sets up scroll listener + initial render.
Virtualizer.update({totalCount, scrollToIndex?}) repaints when filter changes.
Virtualizer.detach() removes listeners and clears state.
Renders only (visibleEnd - visibleStart + buffer) DOM nodes; off-screen cards are not in the DOM.
Verified in browser using playwright: scroll 10k tracks at 60 fps via DevTools perf trace.
Vitest test in tests/web/virtualization.test.js covers attach/update/detach and visible-window correctness.

steps.details: Virtualizer.attach({container, itemHeight, totalCount, renderItem, onWindowChange}). Internal state: scrollTop, visibleStart, visibleEnd, bufferTop=5, bufferBottom=5. Container's child is a tall spacer div height = totalCount * itemHeight; visible cards are positioned absolutely.

container.addEventListener('scroll', _onScroll, {passive: true}). _onScroll computes newVisibleStart = Math.floor(scrollTop / itemHeight). If changed by >=1 row, schedule a render via requestAnimationFrame (coalesce multiple scroll events into 1 RAF callback).

_render() computes start = max(0, visibleStart - bufferTop), end = min(totalCount, visibleEnd + bufferBottom). Calls renderItem(index, recycledNode) for each. Positions each card via element.style.transform = `translateY(${index * itemHeight}px)` (cheap; avoids reflow).
Virtualizer.update({totalCount, scrollToIndex?}). Updates totalCount + spacer height; if scrollToIndex, scrolls to that row; re-renders the visible window.
Remove scroll listener; clear card pool; reset state. Used on tab switch or library reload.
tests/web/virtualization.test.js: stub a container with explicit scrollTop; assert visible window is correct (start/end indices); assert update() with smaller totalCount removes off-screen cards.
tests/e2e/qa-smoke.spec.ts: load app with 10k mock tracks; scroll to bottom; assert document.querySelectorAll('.card').length is bounded (< 100, not 10000); assert no exceptions.

technicalNotes: translateY positioning skips layout - critical for 60fps. Don't use top/left.
passive: true on scroll listener tells the browser the handler won't preventDefault, enabling fast scroll.
Don't store card-to-content mapping in attributes; use a Map<DOMNode, content_id> kept in Virtualizer state.
